name: Bentakoerreka - Pagasarri Trail
name_original: Bentako Erreka, Belaus Kanpaneta, Markio – circular desde Zollo
description: This difficult 14.7-mile mountain biking trail near Bilbao offers challenging terrain, significant elevation gain, and incredible city views
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Arrancudiaga, a municipality in Biscay, Basque Country, Spain, offer diverse terrain for outdoor enthusiasts. The region is characterized by its mountainous surroundings, including significant peaks like Ganekogorta and Gallarraga, and the presence of the Nervión River. Varied elevations and natural trails provide a range of mountain biking experiences, from challenging climbs to scenic descents. Best mountain bike trails around Arrancudiaga The most popular mountain bike trail is Bentakoerreka - Pagasarri Trail, a 14.7 miles (23.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain towards the Pagasarri peak. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Saldropo Wetland – Arteako Benta loop from Ugao-Miraballes, a difficult 41.6 miles (67.0 km) path. This extensive loop offers varied terrain and panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Kurutzegan Menhir – Aranguren Tower loop from Laudio, a 25.4 miles (40.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with notable cultural landmarks, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Mountain biking around Arrancudiaga is defined by mountainous terrains, varied elevations, and natural trails alongside the Nervión River. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Arrancudiaga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 5,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Arrancudiaga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Arrancudiaga?
A: There are nearly 170 mountain bike trails around Arrancudiaga, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels. This includes 15 easy routes, 77 moderate routes, and 75 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Arrancudiaga?
A: Mountain biking around Arrancudiaga is characterized by mountainous terrains, varied elevations, and natural trails. You'll find challenging climbs towards peaks like Ganekogorta and Gallarraga, as well as scenic descents and paths alongside the Nervión River.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Arrancudiaga offers 15 easy mountain bike routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31912519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Easy River loop from Ugao-Miraballes&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.1 km long with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for a relaxed ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Arrancudiaga?
A: The mountain bike trails in Arrancudiaga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the panoramic views from the summits.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its spectacular viewpoints. Trails often lead to summits like Ganekogorta, Gallarraga, and Mandoia, where you can enjoy magnificent panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes, and on clear days, even the city and the sea.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical landmarks along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate cultural landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24715635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kurutzegan Menhir – Aranguren Tower loop from Laudio&lt;/a&gt; leads through mountainous terrain with notable cultural sites. Additionally, you might encounter attractions like the Andra Mari Church or the Iberlandako Bridge in the wider area.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. A notable example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40343317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saldropo Wetland – Arteako Benta loop from Ugao-Miraballes&lt;/a&gt;, which is 67 km long and offers varied terrain and extensive views.

Q: What kind of natural features can I explore while mountain biking in Arrancudiaga?
A: Beyond the mountainous terrain, you can explore features like the Nervión River, which flows through the municipality, and the nearby peaks that form part of the Ganekogorta massif. Wild ponies can sometimes be spotted near the Ganekogorta Summit.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several points of interest. You can find beautiful lakes and reservoirs such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2856350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorostiza Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5530640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kurtzeta Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Cultural highlights in the broader area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/335425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guggenheim Museum&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2911339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zubizuri Bridge&lt;/a&gt; in Bilbao.

Q: Are there any particularly long or challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the area offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24760036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butrón Castle – Karabezu Summit loop from Ugao-Miraballes&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 101.8 km route with over 1200 meters of elevation gain, providing a significant test of endurance.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Arrancudiaga?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, the Basque Country generally experiences mild temperatures, making spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking. These seasons offer pleasant weather and vibrant natural scenery, avoiding the peak heat of summer or potential winter conditions at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by wetlands or unique natural habitats?
A: Yes, some routes offer diverse natural environments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40343317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saldropo Wetland – Arteako Benta loop from Ugao-Miraballes&lt;/a&gt;, for example, takes you through varied terrain that includes wetland areas, providing a different kind of natural experience.
tips: This is a mostly wooded section of the trail. It is wide and easy to follow, but sometimes steep and rocky. If it hasn't been cleaned, it may be a little covered.

This is a beautiful section of the trail that goes along the ridge. It has spectacular views and is even paved in some sections. If you're mountain biking there are several smaller singletrack trails that parallel it and are fun on the way down as well.

This mountain pass has several nice trails leading off it. The main trail goes up the ridge, but there are also some good singletrack roads nearby. If you head towards Markio the path is wide and fairly easy to follow.
Markio is just a house and a small chapel. It is quite isolated, but it is an interesting place to pass by mountain bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Bentakoerreka - Pagasarri Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a diverse mix of surfaces, including gravel and dirt tracks. Riders can expect loose terrain with considerable stones, which may cause wheels to slip, especially on ascents. Some sections might be muddy, particularly in shady areas. While the main path to Pagasarri is often a service road, numerous side trails, including singletrack options, wind through the area. Be prepared for some very steep sections that may require you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for advanced mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Bentakoerreka - Pagasarri Trail is classified as difficult and is best suited for advanced mountain bikers. It demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills due to significant elevation gain, steep sections, and challenging terrain. Some parts, like a 5 km stretch with an average gradient of 8.9%, are considered quite tough.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Bentakoerreka - Pagasarri Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations aren't detailed, the trail is accessible from areas like Zollo and Markio. Given its popularity, especially near Pagasarri, look for designated parking areas or consider starting points that offer easier access to the trail network.

faqs.question: How can I reach the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: The Pagasarri area is a popular destination near Bilbao, suggesting public transport options might be available to reach the general vicinity. For precise trailhead access, it's recommended to check local bus routes or train services that connect to towns like Zollo or Markio, which are common starting points for routes in this area.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect incredible views of the city of Bilbao and the surrounding mountains. The area is characterized by lush greenery and a spectacular forest environment. Near the summit, there's a park area with picnic tables, perfect for taking in the vistas. On foggy days, the landscape can transform into a magical and romantic atmosphere.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest or landmarks on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the route, you'll encounter several points of interest. These include the Bentako Erreka Trail, the Crest Ridge Path, and the Mandabide Mountain Pass. You can also pass by the Markio Chapel. Near the summit of Pagasarri, there's also a mountain refuge with a bar and restaurant.

faqs.question: Is the Bentakoerreka - Pagasarri Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Pagasarri area is generally popular for outdoor activities, specific rules for dogs on mountain biking trails can vary. It's common for dogs to be allowed, but always ensure they are kept under control, especially given the potential for encountering local wildlife like cows, goats, horses, or even a bull. Leash rules should be observed.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable when dry, so spring and autumn typically offer the best conditions, avoiding the wettest winter months and potentially very hot summer days. Be aware that some sections can be muddy, especially in shady areas, so checking recent weather conditions is advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride the trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Bentakoerreka - Pagasarri Trail for mountain biking. It is a popular public access area. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations and private property if venturing off marked paths.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other routes in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other well-known paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Bentakoerreka - Pagasarri trail itself (66% of the route), as well as smaller segments of "Atzoko bideak, gaurko bidaiariak" (9%) and "Seberetxe - Campa de Txus" (2%). Additionally, various sections named "Bentako Erreka" (37%, 10%, 7%, 2%) are part of this extensive network.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a mountain refuge conveniently located near the summit of Pagasarri that features a bar and restaurant. This provides an excellent resting point for visitors to refuel and enjoy the views.
